# Build: Slice 5 — Promotion + docs + version

Spec reference: `docs/spec-relay_interfaces.md` (Slice 5 sub-checklist lines 83-102; Doc updates lines 514-535; Definition of done lines 571-585; Decision 5 lines 333-342 for lifecycle, Decision 6 line 350 for empty-tuple bit-for-bit identity)
Status: final-accepted

## Plan (Worker 1)

### DRY analysis

- **Existing patterns reused.**
  - `DEFERRED_META_KEYS` / `ALLOWED_META_KEYS` constants in `django_strawberry_framework/types/base.py:43-73` are the single literal-tuple site for the validator's deferred/allowed split. The promotion is a one-line move (remove `"interfaces"` plus its 13-line block comment from `DEFERRED_META_KEYS`, add `"interfaces"` to `ALLOWED_META_KEYS`); no validator code changes. `_validate_meta` already runs `_validate_interfaces` after the deferred-key check, so the moment the literal moves the end-to-end path lights up without further wiring (carried-forward Slice 1/3 memory: source of truth for "is `relay.Node` declared?" is `definition.interfaces`).
  - The `__version__ = "0.0.4"` -> `"0.0.5"` bump pattern is identical to `DONE-008` (KANBAN.md lines 269-286). Three sites move together: `pyproject.toml:4`, `django_strawberry_framework/__init__.py:24`, and the assertion in `tests/base/test_init.py:11`. The TODO anchor at `__init__.py:25-29` (which names `docs/spec-relay_interfaces.md` and promises only `__version__` changes, no new exports) gets removed in the same change — that is the matching TODO removal called out by Slice 1's pattern.
  - `tests/base/test_init.py::test_public_api_surface_is_pinned` (lines 30-42) already pins the six-name public surface; this assertion is the regression guard for Definition-of-done item 11 ("No new public exports"). It needs no change in Slice 5 — confirming it remains untouched is what proves the no-new-exports contract is honored. The only test edit is the version-string assertion at `tests/base/test_init.py:11`.
  - The `_decode_global_id(global_id)` HTTP helper at `examples/fakeshop/test_query/test_library_api.py:500-509` and the `test_library_relay_node_global_id_round_trips` HTTP test (line 545+) already exercise an end-to-end Relay-declared `library` model via the bypass. The fakeshop guidance update in `TODAY.md` should describe the same `GenreType` shape this HTTP test exercises so the docs and the test agree on one canonical example.
  - Doc-shape DRY across `docs/FEATURES.md` / `docs/README.md` / `TODAY.md` / `KANBAN.md` / `CHANGELOG.md`: each restates a piece of the same story (what Slice 5 ships). Single canonical phrasings per concept land identically across all five docs to prevent prose drift. See "Implementation steps" below for the canonical strings.

- **New helpers justified.** None. Slice 5 is a constant-promotion + doc + version slice; no code logic, no new module, no new helper. Static-inspection helper skipped (the spec defaults the helper to "required for new logic in `types/`"; the promotion is a literal-tuple membership move below the 30-line threshold and adds no logic — record "promotion only; no new logic added" as the helper skip in the artifact).

- **Duplication risk avoided.**
  - **Drift risk in cross-doc phrasing.** Five files (`docs/FEATURES.md`, `docs/README.md`, `TODAY.md`, `KANBAN.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`) each describe the same shipped scope. Without one canonical phrasing per concept, the descriptions will drift between docs over the next slice or two. Pin canonical strings now and reuse:
    - **Subsystem name:** "Relay Node integration" (used as the `docs/FEATURES.md` subsection header, the `docs/README.md` quick-start subsection header, and the `KANBAN.md` `DONE-011` card title).
    - **Resolver list literal:** "`resolve_id_attr`, `resolve_id`, `resolve_node`, `resolve_nodes`" (in that order — matches `_RELAY_RESOLVER_DEFAULTS` at `types/relay.py:297-302` per the Slice 4 carry-forward; do NOT reorder).
    - **Composite-pk constraint phrasing:** "Models whose primary key is a Django 5.2+ `CompositePrimaryKey` raise `ConfigurationError` at finalization; declare an explicit `id: relay.NodeID[...]` annotation or remove `relay.Node` from `Meta.interfaces` to remediate." Reuse verbatim in `docs/FEATURES.md` (Relay Node integration subsection) and `CHANGELOG.md` `### Added` line for the composite-pk rejection.
    - **`is_type_of` scope phrasing:** "`is_type_of` injection is unconditional for every `DjangoType` (Relay-declared or not); consumer-declared `is_type_of` is preserved." Reuse in `CHANGELOG.md` `### Added` and `docs/FEATURES.md` Relay Node integration subsection.
    - **Promotion-line phrasing:** "`Meta.interfaces` promoted from `DEFERRED_META_KEYS` to `ALLOWED_META_KEYS`." Lands once in `CHANGELOG.md` `### Changed`.
    - **Version-bump line in CHANGELOG header:** `## [0.0.5] - 2026-05-13` (today's date per env block).
  - **Promotion-atomicity risk.** The promotion at `types/base.py:43-73` is one literal move that lights up the end-to-end `Meta.interfaces` path through `_validate_meta`. Until that move lands, every test that bypasses the validator (via `tests/_relay_bypass.py`) passes; after, the bypass becomes redundant. Worker 2 must do the promotion + bypass deletion + bypass call-site replacement + version bump in one change. Splitting them risks an intermediate state where the validator rejects the now-redundant bypass's stale call sites.
  - **Bypass-replacement uniformity.** The 26 `stage_relay_definition(...)` call sites across 5 test files all follow the same shape: register a plain `DjangoType` (no `Meta.interfaces`), call `stage_relay_definition(CategoryNode)` (or `(CategoryNode, interfaces=(Auditable,))`), then `finalize_django_types()`. Replacement is a uniform conversion: move `interfaces = (relay.Node,)` (or `interfaces = (Auditable,)`) into the `class Meta:` block, drop the `stage_relay_definition(...)` line, drop the `from tests._relay_bypass import stage_relay_definition` line. The two unit-boundary tests `test_apply_interfaces_skips_already_present_bases` (line 771) and `test_apply_interfaces_wraps_typeerror_as_configuration_error` (line 792) do not use `stage_relay_definition`; they construct synthetic `_Host` classes and call `apply_interfaces` directly. Those tests stay untouched.

- **`tests/_relay_bypass.py` keep-or-delete decision: DELETE.** The bypass exists for one reason (per its module docstring lines 1-28): "Slice 4 cannot declare `Meta.interfaces = (relay.Node,)` end-to-end while `"interfaces"` is still in `DEFERRED_META_KEYS` (Slice 5 promotes the key)." Slice 5 promotes the key. All 26 call sites across 5 files (`tests/test_registry.py`, `tests/types/test_definition_order_schema.py`, `tests/types/test_relay_interfaces.py`, `tests/optimizer/test_relay_id_projection.py`, `examples/fakeshop/test_query/test_library_api.py`) follow a uniform replacement shape (see Duplication risk avoided above). The two unit-boundary tests (`test_apply_interfaces_skips_already_present_bases`, `test_apply_interfaces_wraps_typeerror_as_configuration_error`) call `apply_interfaces` directly on a synthetic `_Host` without `stage_relay_definition`, so they continue to exercise the unit boundary cleanly after deletion. The HTTP test's `_reload_with_library_relay_node` helper at `examples/fakeshop/test_query/test_library_api.py:512-541` collapses cleanly: the bypass call between the library-schema reload and the project-schema reload disappears once `GenreType` declares `interfaces = (relay.Node,)` in its `Meta` block. Worker 1's Slice 4 carry-forward memory entry called this out explicitly ("Slice 5's promotion of `"interfaces"` collapses [the bypass] to a one-file removal — Slice 5 plan must explicitly name this cleanup"); this is that name. **Decision: delete `tests/_relay_bypass.py`, convert all 26 call sites to end-to-end declaration in `class Meta`.**

- **Post-promotion test additions decision.** Slice 3's Worker 3 Low-priority follow-up flagged a schema-level `cls.__annotations__["id"]` and `id: GlobalID!` assertion once `"interfaces"` is promoted. Slice 4's `test_relay_declared_type_emits_node_interface_and_global_id` (in `tests/types/test_definition_order_schema.py:101-123`) already asserts the schema-level `id: ID!` field via the bypass. Once Slice 5 deletes the bypass and that test converts to end-to-end declaration, the schema-level assertion is exercised against the real `Meta.interfaces = (relay.Node,)` path — the Slice 4 coverage becomes the post-promotion coverage automatically. No new test file is needed; the conversion of the existing test from bypass to end-to-end IS the post-promotion end-to-end coverage. The Slice 4 carry-forward predicted this exact outcome.

### Implementation steps

1. **Promote `"interfaces"` out of the deferred set.** `django_strawberry_framework/types/base.py:43-73`:
   - Remove `"interfaces"` from the `DEFERRED_META_KEYS` frozenset (currently the last entry at line 60; remove the entry).
   - Remove the 13-line block comment at `types/base.py:48-59` explaining why `"interfaces"` is deferred. The comment becomes false the moment the entry moves.
   - Remove the TODO anchor at the bottom of that comment (lines 56-59) — its condition (this slice landing) is now true. AGENTS.md "Design docs and TODO anchors" says remove the anchor in the same change that ships the slice.
   - Add `"interfaces"` to the `ALLOWED_META_KEYS` frozenset (currently the six entries at lines 64-73). Insert it in alphabetical position or trailing — the existing literal order is `model, fields, exclude, name, description, optimizer_hints` (declaration order, not alphabetical), so append `"interfaces"` at the end of the frozenset literal to match the existing convention. Keep the trailing comma on the last argument per AGENTS.md "Formatting and lint" (COM812).

2. **Bump the package version.** Three sites move together, exactly as `DONE-008` did the `0.0.3` -> `0.0.4` bump (KANBAN.md lines 269-286):
   - `pyproject.toml:4`: `version = "0.0.4"` -> `version = "0.0.5"`.
   - `django_strawberry_framework/__init__.py:24`: `__version__ = "0.0.4"` -> `__version__ = "0.0.5"`.
   - `django_strawberry_framework/__init__.py:25-29`: remove the TODO anchor (its condition is now satisfied — this slice is the promised `0.0.5` bump with no new exports).
   - `tests/base/test_init.py:11`: `assert __version__ == "0.0.4"` -> `assert __version__ == "0.0.5"`.
   - Regenerate `uv.lock` by running `uv lock` at the repo root (Worker 2 runs this; the lockfile is tracked).
   - Do NOT touch `tests/base/test_init.py:30-42` (`test_public_api_surface_is_pinned`); it must remain unchanged to prove Definition-of-done item 11 ("No new public exports").

3. **Delete `tests/_relay_bypass.py` and convert all call sites to end-to-end declaration.** Delete the module file. Then in each of the 5 call-site files, perform the uniform transformation: drop the `from tests._relay_bypass import stage_relay_definition` import; for each `stage_relay_definition(TypeName)` call, move `interfaces = (relay.Node,)` into the type's `class Meta:` block as a new line right after the `fields = (...)` tuple, then delete the `stage_relay_definition(TypeName)` line. For the one variant call `stage_relay_definition(CategoryNode, interfaces=(Auditable,))` in `test_non_relay_interface_works` at `tests/types/test_relay_interfaces.py:762`, the conversion is `interfaces = (Auditable,)`. The two unit-boundary tests `test_apply_interfaces_skips_already_present_bases` (`tests/types/test_relay_interfaces.py:771`) and `test_apply_interfaces_wraps_typeerror_as_configuration_error` (`:792`) stay untouched — they call `apply_interfaces` directly on synthetic `_Host` classes without `stage_relay_definition`. The 5 call-site files are:
   - `tests/test_registry.py:26, 385, 402` — 2 call sites in `test_registry_clear_allows_fresh_relay_declared_type_to_finalize`.
   - `tests/types/test_definition_order_schema.py:11, 109, 139` — 2 call sites in `test_relay_declared_type_emits_node_interface_and_global_id` and `test_mixed_relay_and_non_relay_types_introspect_cleanly`. The mixed-test case has only the Relay type staged; convert that one and leave `ItemType` non-Relay.
   - `tests/types/test_relay_interfaces.py:41` and 20+ call sites throughout the file (lines 219, 385, 402, 419, 443, 464, 483, 499, 529, 551, 574, 646, 664, 682, 700, 718, 745, 762, 825, 847, 865, 888, 931).
   - `tests/optimizer/test_relay_id_projection.py:27, 48, 76, 108, 140` — 4 call sites (one per test function).
   - `examples/fakeshop/test_query/test_library_api.py:15, 516 (docstring mention), 530` — replace the `stage_relay_definition(library_schema.GenreType)` call between the library-schema reload and the project-schema reload with: declare `interfaces = (relay.Node,)` in `apps/library/schema.py`'s `GenreType.Meta` block (see step 5 below). Update the helper's docstring to reflect that the reload chain no longer needs the bypass.
   - Update the module docstring of `tests/types/test_relay_interfaces.py` (lines 1-17) to drop the explanation of "until Slice 5 promotes" — that explanation is now historical and confusing. New text: short paragraph naming the slice scope (Relay interfaces, `Meta.interfaces` validation, `is_type_of` injection, id suppression, base injection, four resolver defaults).

4. **Delete the lingering spec draft.** `docs/spec-relay_interfaces-3.md` — delete the file. Per the active spec's status line at line 3 ("drafts `-1.md` and `-2.md` have already been removed and only `docs/spec-relay_interfaces-3.md` remains to be deleted as part of this slice"), this is the explicit cleanup the spec sub-checklist names at line 97.

5. **Update `examples/fakeshop/apps/library/schema.py::GenreType` to declare `Meta.interfaces`.** Currently `GenreType` at lines 51-56 has `class Meta: model = models.Genre; fields = ("id", "name", "books")`. Add `interfaces = (relay.Node,)` after the `fields` tuple. Add the top-level import `from strawberry import relay` next to the existing `import strawberry` at the top of the file. This converts the HTTP test's bypass-staged `library` model into a real consumer-facing Relay-declared type — the HTTP test continues to pass without modification (apart from dropping its bypass call per step 3).

6. **Update `docs/FEATURES.md`.** Three changes, using canonical phrasings from the DRY analysis:
   - Line 20: `Current package version: \`0.0.4\`.` -> `Current package version: \`0.0.5\`.`
   - Lines 60-65 (the "Planned" list under "DRF-shaped GraphQL API"): remove the `Meta.interfaces` bullet (line 63). Leave the other planned-key bullets in place.
   - Lines 134-141 (the "Deferred field conversion" list): remove the `Relay GlobalID mapping for auto IDs` bullet (line 139). The other deferred bullets stay.
   - Lines 372-382 (the "Deferred and future work" list): remove `Relay interfaces and \`GlobalID\`` bullet (line 377). The other bullets stay.
   - Insert a new shipped subsection after the "DRF-shaped GraphQL API" section's existing "Planned" block (around line 65, before "## Django model to Strawberry type generation" at line 67). Title: `### Relay Node integration` (canonical name from DRY). Body: a one-paragraph overview describing the declarative `Meta.interfaces = (relay.Node,)` shape, the four `resolve_*` methods (in canonical order from DRY: `resolve_id_attr, resolve_id, resolve_node, resolve_nodes`), the id-suppression behavior tied to declaring `relay.Node` (one sentence: when `relay.Node` is in `Meta.interfaces`, the synthesized Django `id: int!` is suppressed and the Relay-supplied `id: GlobalID!` from the interface is used instead), the composite-pk constraint (canonical phrasing from DRY: "Models whose primary key is a Django 5.2+ `CompositePrimaryKey` raise `ConfigurationError` at finalization; declare an explicit `id: relay.NodeID[...]` annotation or remove `relay.Node` from `Meta.interfaces` to remediate"), and the unconditional `is_type_of` injection (canonical phrasing from DRY: "`is_type_of` injection is unconditional for every `DjangoType` (Relay-declared or not); consumer-declared `is_type_of` is preserved"). One short code block showing the same shape `docs/README.md`'s Relay Node subsection uses (step 7 below) — keep the example minimal: a single model-backed type with `interfaces = (relay.Node,)`. Status line: `Status: shipped.` Do NOT add Connection / DjangoConnectionField language — that is still planned per `BLOCKED-001` and `NEXT-005`.

7. **Update `docs/README.md`.** Add a "Relay Node" subsection right after the "Quick start" block (after line 50 / before "## Why this package exists" at line 52). Subsection heading: `### Relay Node` (per the spec's "gated behind a 'Relay Node' subsection" wording at line 521; canonical name lands twice, once as `### Relay Node` here and once as `### Relay Node integration` in `docs/FEATURES.md` — both spellings are intentional, the second is the full subsystem name and the first is the gating header). Content: one short paragraph stating that `Meta.interfaces = (relay.Node,)` is the supported way to declare a Relay-node-shaped type, plus a small code block reusing the quick-start's `CategoryType` import surface but adding `from strawberry import relay` and `interfaces = (relay.Node,)` to one `class Meta:` block. End with a one-line pointer: "See [`FEATURES.md`'s Relay Node integration subsection](FEATURES.md#relay-node-integration) for the resolver list, composite-pk constraint, and the `is_type_of` injection contract." Also update line 100 (the "Expect the deferred `Meta` keys ..." paragraph): drop `interfaces` from the deferred-keys list (currently the last entry: `\`filterset_class\`, \`orderset_class\`, \`aggregate_class\`, \`fields_class\`, \`search_fields\`, \`interfaces\``). Update line 98's "Status: 0.0.4" line to "Status: 0.0.5".

8. **Update `TODAY.md`.** Two changes:

The four other entries (`DjangoConnectionField`, `apply_cascade_permissions`, the four `Meta.<*set>_class` keys, `Meta.search_fields`) stay.
   - Lines 234-242 ("What the fakeshop example should wait for"): remove `Relay node and connection support` (line 238). The four other "wait for" entries (`DjangoConnectionField`, filters/ordering/aggregates/fieldsets, search fields, permission cascade helpers) stay.

Keep the products-catalog "list-based schema" guidance unchanged — products still don't use `relay.Node`; only `library.GenreType` does (after step 5 lands).

9. **Update `KANBAN.md`.** Two changes:
   - Lines 326-414 (the `IN-PROGRESS-001 — Relay interfaces and Node foundation (0.0.5)` card): convert to a new `DONE-011` card under the `## Done` section (after `DONE-010` at line 306). Card title: `DONE-011 — 0.0.5 Relay interfaces and Node foundation` (canonical "Relay Node" phrasing from DRY; the long-form "Relay interfaces and Node foundation" matches the spec title). Status: `complete.` Scope bullets condense the spec's five slices into shipped-state bullets reusing the canonical phrasings:
     - `Meta.interfaces` accepted end-to-end for any Strawberry interface.
     - Four Relay node resolver defaults injected when `relay.Node` is declared (canonical order: `resolve_id_attr`, `resolve_id`, `resolve_node`, `resolve_nodes`).

     - Composite-primary-key rejection (canonical phrasing from DRY).
     - Both sync and async paths for `_resolve_node_default` / `_resolve_nodes_default`.
   - Evidence bullets: the implementation files (`django_strawberry_framework/types/base.py`, `types/relay.py`, `types/finalizer.py`), the test files (`tests/types/test_relay_interfaces.py`, `tests/types/test_definition_order_schema.py`, `tests/optimizer/test_relay_id_projection.py`, `tests/test_registry.py`, `examples/fakeshop/test_query/test_library_api.py`), the doc updates (`CHANGELOG.md`, `docs/FEATURES.md`, `docs/README.md`, `TODAY.md`), and the example-app touch (`examples/fakeshop/apps/library/schema.py` — `GenreType` declares `Meta.interfaces = (relay.Node,)`).
   - Notes bullets: borrowed patterns from `strawberry-django` (cite the spec's "Borrowing posture" section at lines 149-159 and Decision 3 at lines 288-317); the override discriminator triad (`__dict__` for `is_type_of`, `relay.Node in interfaces` tuple-membership for id suppression, `__func__` identity for the four `resolve_*` defaults) stays distinct across the three injection sites.
   - Update lines 38-41 ("In progress" section): remove the `IN-PROGRESS-001` mention and the strategic-roadmap line. The "In progress" section becomes empty or absorbs the next active slice if one is on deck; if empty, change the section header note to reflect that.
   - Lines 1035-1047 (the "Recommended hybrid (current direction)" sequence): step 1 ("**Active** — `IN-PROGRESS-001` Relay / `Meta.interfaces` (`0.0.5`)") becomes "**Next** — `NEXT-001` `FieldSet`" (renumber the remaining steps down by one). Keep the same overall hybrid order; the change is just dropping the now-shipped Relay step and shifting the sequence forward. The successor card cross-reference at line 414 ("Successor card: when this lands, move to `DONE-011` ...") is now the literal action being recorded — that line collapses into the new DONE-011 card.
   - Lines 39 ("In progress" intro paragraph) and 56 (the "Meta.interfaces / Relay interface wiring is now the active 0.0.5 slice" callout) and lines 968-977 (`BLOCKED-001 — Full Relay story`): update each to reflect that Relay Node is now shipped (Connection field stays blocked on `NEXT-005`). `BLOCKED-001` should split into shipped-Node and still-blocked-Connection — preserve the connection-field blocker but mark Node as unblocked.

10. **Update `CHANGELOG.md`.** Add a new `## [0.0.5] - 2026-05-13` entry under the existing `## [Unreleased]` placeholder at line 8 (above the `## [0.0.4]` entry at line 10). Section blocks (in this order — matches Keep a Changelog and the existing `0.0.4` entry shape):
    - `### Added` block — reuse canonical phrasings:
      - Relay Node interface support: `Meta.interfaces` accepted for any Strawberry interface (Relay `Node` or `@strawberry.interface`-decorated classes).
      - Default `resolve_id_attr`, `resolve_id`, `resolve_node`, `resolve_nodes` classmethods injected on every `DjangoType` whose `Meta.interfaces` declares `relay.Node`; consumer-declared overrides are preserved via Strawberry's `__func__` identity test (matches `strawberry-django`).
      - Automatic synthesized `id: int!` suppression when `relay.Node` is in `Meta.interfaces`; the Relay-supplied `id: GlobalID!` from the interface is used instead. The Django primary key remains selected as a connector column for the optimizer.
      - `is_type_of` injection (canonical phrasing from DRY).
      - Composite-primary-key rejection (canonical phrasing from DRY).
      - Both sync and async paths for `_resolve_node_default` / `_resolve_nodes_default`; `_resolve_id_attr_default` and `_resolve_id_default` are sync.
    - `### Changed` block — reuse canonical phrasing: "`Meta.interfaces` promoted from `DEFERRED_META_KEYS` to `ALLOWED_META_KEYS`."
    - Version bump line: today's date in the heading.
    - No `### Fixed` or `### Removed` entries unless the implementation pass surfaces something (per spec line 534: "as needed by the implementation"). If Worker 2 deletes `tests/_relay_bypass.py` and updates the example app's `GenreType` declaration, those are internal test/example changes and do not belong in the consumer-facing changelog.

11. **Final gates.** Worker 2 runs (after every edit per `START.md`):
    - `uv run ruff format .` — must pass.
    - `uv run ruff check --fix .` — must pass.
    - `uv run pytest` — full sweep across all three test trees (per `AGENTS.md` "Test placement"). The spec's Slice 5 final gate at line 101 explicitly includes the full sweep because the promotion changes the test-corpus shape (the bypass disappears; every former bypass site now exercises the end-to-end validator path). This is partially redundant with the build-closing `bld-final.md` gate that Worker 1 runs after Slice 5 closes; the redundancy is intentional. Worker 2 runs the pytest gate inside Slice 5 to verify the promotion landed cleanly before handing off; Worker 1 re-runs it at `bld-final.md` as the cycle-closing gate after the integration pass also runs. Both runs are 100%-coverage runs because `pyproject.toml [tool.coverage.report] fail_under = 100` is always on.
    - Confirm `tests/base/test_init.py::test_public_api_surface_is_pinned` still passes unchanged — this is the proof of Definition-of-done item 11 ("No new public exports").

### Test additions / updates

- **Version assertion.** `tests/base/test_init.py:11`: update the assertion string to `"0.0.5"`. No new test.

- **Public-surface pin (no edit needed).** `tests/base/test_init.py:30-42` (`test_public_api_surface_is_pinned`) already pins the six-name surface. It must remain untouched and pass — confirmation of Definition-of-done item 11.

- **Schema-level post-promotion coverage (no new file needed; conversion).** `tests/types/test_definition_order_schema.py::test_relay_declared_type_emits_node_interface_and_global_id` (line 101) already asserts the schema-level `id: ID!` field via the bypass. After step 3's conversion, this test exercises the post-promotion end-to-end path (`Meta.interfaces = (relay.Node,)` declared on the consumer class, validated by `_validate_meta`, stored on `DjangoTypeDefinition.interfaces`, injected at Phase 2.5, rendered as `id: GlobalID!` by `strawberry.type(cls, ...)`). Slice 3's Worker 3 Low-priority follow-up is satisfied by this conversion; no new test is needed.

- **End-to-end `Meta.interfaces` validation coverage.** Every `_validate_interfaces` test in `tests/types/test_relay_interfaces.py:62-198` currently calls `_validate_interfaces(meta)` directly (because the deferred-key check short-circuits before reaching the validator). After the promotion lands, the end-to-end `class Foo(DjangoType): class Meta: interfaces = (relay.Node,)` path runs through `_validate_meta` for the first time. The existing direct-call tests still pass (the helper signature is unchanged), AND every step-3 bypass-replacement test now also exercises the end-to-end validator path implicitly. Optional Worker 2 addition: a single new `test_meta_interfaces_end_to_end_accepted_in_validate_meta` test that exercises the full `class Foo(DjangoType): class Meta: interfaces = (relay.Node,)` path through `__init_subclass__` and asserts `Foo.__django_strawberry_definition__.interfaces == (relay.Node,)`. This proves the promotion lit up the validator without being shadowed by the deferred-key gate. Recommended.

- **Bypass deletion verification.** No new test. The bypass deletion is verified by `uv run pytest` passing on the converted call sites — if any conversion missed a site, the test fails with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests._relay_bypass'` or `NameError: name 'stage_relay_definition' is not defined`. The build pipeline catches this without a dedicated test.

- **Temp-test note for Worker 3.** Worker 3 may want to construct a temp test under `docs/build/temp-tests/slice-5/` that asserts `"interfaces"` is now in `ALLOWED_META_KEYS` and absent from `DEFERRED_META_KEYS` as a literal-tuple membership test — a one-line sanity check. Optional; the bigger end-to-end test in `tests/types/test_relay_interfaces.py` covers the same ground at a higher level.

### Open questions for Worker 2

1. **Order of operations within the change.** The plan lists steps 1-11 in a logical order, but the change is one PR and Worker 2 may interleave (e.g., do the version bump first to confirm `uv lock` regenerates cleanly, then do the promotion and the bypass deletion together). The constraint is atomicity: the promotion at step 1 and the bypass deletion at step 3 must land in the same change, because the bypass becomes structurally redundant the moment the promotion lands. Worker 2's discretion on intra-change ordering.

2. **`docs/FEATURES.md` Relay Node integration subsection placement.** The plan suggests placing the new subsection between "Planned" (line 65) and "## Django model to Strawberry type generation" (line 67) under the "DRF-shaped GraphQL API" parent section. An alternative is to add it as a sibling top-level section (`## Relay Node integration`) elsewhere in the file. Worker 2's call — the subsection is shorter than the top-level section and the surrounding shipped-then-planned shape of the parent section reads more cleanly with the subsection inside it. Recommended: subsection inside "DRF-shaped GraphQL API".

3. **The `### Removed` changelog block for the deleted bypass.** Slice 5 deletes `tests/_relay_bypass.py`, which is internal test scaffolding (not part of the consumer-facing package surface). The `0.0.4` changelog `### Removed` entry ("Removed the unused `TypeRegistry.lazy_ref` placeholder ...") is also internal but lands in the changelog because it touches the public registry surface. The bypass is purely test-only and never reachable via package imports. Recommendation: do NOT add a `### Removed` entry for the bypass deletion. Worker 2's call.

4. **`KANBAN.md` `BLOCKED-001` split.** The "Full Relay story" card at lines 965-977 lists three blockers (`Meta.interfaces` design, `GlobalID` mapping decision, `DjangoConnectionField` design). The first two are now resolved; the third is still blocked. Worker 2's call on whether to split the card into a "DONE — Node half" plus "BLOCKED — Connection half" or just update the existing card to reflect partial resolution. Recommendation: update in place with the resolved-blockers struck through, since the unblocked outcomes (fakeshop aspirational schema activation, Relay node queries) are now partly unblocked.

5. **`apps/library/schema.py::GenreType` Meta key ordering.** Currently the `Meta` block is `model = ...; fields = (...)`. Adding `interfaces = (relay.Node,)` could land before or after the `fields` tuple. The existing fakeshop convention isn't pinned anywhere. Recommendation: after `fields` (matches the spec's example block at lines 184-188) so the layout reads "what model, which fields, which interfaces."

6. **Module-docstring rewrite vs. trim.** `tests/types/test_relay_interfaces.py:1-17` currently explains why the file calls `_validate_interfaces` directly and uses `stage_relay_definition` — both rationales become historical after step 3. Worker 2 may either delete the explanation entirely (the file's purpose is self-evident from its name) or rewrite it as a short statement of scope. Recommendation: rewrite to one or two short sentences naming the slice scope.

- **Promotion site.** `types/base.py:43-62` is now two compact frozenset literals; the 13-line deferred-block comment and its TODO anchor are gone. The `_validate_meta` docstring step-5 wording was also trimmed.

Manual touch-ups handled the one `stage_relay_definition(CategoryNode, interfaces=(Auditable,))` variant and the docstring trims. All call sites verified gone via `grep -c "stage_relay_definition"`.
- **`test_install_relay_node_resolvers_preserves_consumer_override` (formerly the one site that did NOT call `finalize_django_types()` after the bypass).** After conversion, this test now declares `Meta.interfaces = (relay.Node,)` end-to-end, but it still manually appends `relay.Node` to `__bases__` and calls `install_relay_node_resolvers(CategoryNode)` directly because the goal is to exercise the install helper without running the full finalizer. The end-to-end declaration causes `__init_subclass__` to validate and store the tuple on the definition; Phase 2.5 (which would otherwise apply the interface bases and call `install_relay_node_resolvers`) is not invoked because `finalize_django_types()` is never called. The manual `__bases__` append + `install_relay_node_resolvers(CategoryNode)` call still drives the install helper through its consumer-override branch. The test passes.
- **Composite-pk test (`test_relay_node_with_composite_pk_raises`).** Converted to declare `Meta.interfaces = (relay.Node,)` end-to-end. The monkey-patch of `Category._meta.pk` runs after class creation, so `__init_subclass__` validates the tuple against the real (non-composite) pk; the composite-pk rejection then fires at finalize time inside Phase 2.5. The test passes.
- **`docs/FEATURES.md` Relay Node integration subsection placement.** Placed it under the "DRF-shaped GraphQL API" parent section, between the "Planned" list and the "## Django model to Strawberry type generation" sibling section (Open Question 2's recommended option). Reads cleanly inside the shipped-then-planned shape.
- **`### Removed` changelog block.** None added (Open Question 3 recommendation). The bypass deletion is internal test scaffolding; the spec example deletion (`docs/spec-relay_interfaces-3.md`) is internal docs cleanup. Neither is consumer-facing.
- **`KANBAN.md` `BLOCKED-001`.** Updated in place (Open Question 4 recommendation): struck through the two resolved Node-half blockers (`Meta.interfaces` design, `GlobalID` mapping decision) and left the `DjangoConnectionField` blocker active. The "Unblocks" list is annotated so each line carries a `(shipped via DONE-011)` or `(Connection half still blocked)` qualifier.
- **`apps/library/schema.py::GenreType` Meta key ordering.** Placed `interfaces = (relay.Node,)` after the `fields` tuple (Open Question 5 recommendation), matching the spec's example block layout.
- **`tests/types/test_relay_interfaces.py` module docstring.** Rewritten as one short paragraph naming the slice scope (Open Question 6 recommendation).
- **No new public exports.** `django_strawberry_framework/__init__.py` only changed `__version__`; `__all__` is unchanged. `tests/base/test_init.py::test_public_api_surface_is_pinned` passes unchanged.
- **No static-inspection helper run.** Per the plan's DRY-analysis explicit skip note ("promotion only; no new logic added"). The change is a literal-tuple membership move below the 30-line threshold and adds no logic to `django_strawberry_framework/`.

## Final verification (Worker 1)

- **DRY check across all five slices.** Pass. The four-discriminator architecture is intact in the post-promotion tree: Slice 1's `_validate_interfaces` at `django_strawberry_framework/types/base.py:277`, Slice 2's `cls.__dict__` membership check for consumer-declared `is_type_of` at `django_strawberry_framework/types/relay.py:75`, Slice 3's tuple-membership check `relay.Node in interfaces` at `django_strawberry_framework/types/base.py:557`, and Slice 4's `issubclass(type_cls, relay.Node)` MRO check at `django_strawberry_framework/types/relay.py:50` plus the `__func__` identity test at `django_strawberry_framework/types/relay.py:306-328`. Slice 5 is a constant-promotion + docs + version slice and did not collapse, hoist, or generalize any of the four. `_RELAY_RESOLVER_DEFAULTS` remains the sole iteration site for the four method names. No new helper, no near-copy, no generic override detector crept in via the bypass-deletion refactor. Canonical doc phrasings landed verbatim across `docs/FEATURES.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `KANBAN.md`, `docs/README.md`, and `TODAY.md` as planned. `DEFERRED_META_KEYS` and `ALLOWED_META_KEYS` remain two compact frozenset literals — the promoted entry moved, it did not duplicate.
- **Focused tests.** Pass. `uv run pytest tests/types/ tests/optimizer/ tests/test_registry.py tests/base/test_init.py --cov=django_strawberry_framework --cov-report=term-missing` reports **421 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failed**. The reported 97.56% coverage on this focused scope is expected — `conf.py` and `utils/typing.py` are exercised by trees outside this focused command (the build-closing `bld-final.md` full sweep is the cycle's 100% gate; Worker 2 already confirmed `516 passed / 1 skipped / 100% on 1351 statements` end-to-end). Every package file touched by the promotion (`types/base.py`, `types/relay.py`, `types/finalizer.py`, `__init__.py`) reports 100% under this focused scope. The single skip pre-dates this build.
- **Spec reconciliation.** Made. Edited the spec status line at `docs/spec-relay_interfaces.md:3` to remove the now-stale "remains to be deleted as part of this slice" qualifier and assert single-source-of-truth status. `docs/spec-relay_interfaces-3.md` is deleted on disk (confirmed via `git status -- docs/spec-relay_interfaces-3.md`); the spec status line now reflects that reality. Slice 4's prior Decision 3 edit (lines 313-315) is intact. No other spec reconciliation surfaced.
- **Final status.** `final-accepted`.

### Summary

Slice 5 promoted `"interfaces"` from `DEFERRED_META_KEYS` to `ALLOWED_META_KEYS` in one literal-tuple move at `django_strawberry_framework/types/base.py:43-63`, lighting up the end-to-end `Meta.interfaces` path through `_validate_meta` for the first time; bumped the package version to `0.0.5` synchronously across `pyproject.toml`, `django_strawberry_framework/__init__.py`, and `tests/base/test_init.py` and regenerated `uv.lock`; deleted `tests/_relay_bypass.py` and converted all 26 bypass call sites across five test files to end-to-end `Meta.interfaces` declarations (the two unit-boundary tests calling `apply_interfaces` directly stayed untouched); deleted the lingering superseded draft `docs/spec-relay_interfaces-3.md`; updated `examples/fakeshop/apps/library/schema.py::GenreType` to declare `interfaces = (relay.Node,)`; and refreshed the five consumer docs (`docs/FEATURES.md`, `docs/README.md`, `TODAY.md`, `KANBAN.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`) with canonical phrasings for the new Relay Node subsystem. With Slice 5 closed, the `0.0.5` build delivers Relay Node integration end-to-end: `Meta.interfaces` accepted for any Strawberry interface, four Relay resolver defaults (`resolve_id_attr`, `resolve_id`, `resolve_node`, `resolve_nodes`) injected when `relay.Node` is declared (consumer overrides preserved via `__func__` identity), automatic synthesized `id: int!` suppression in favor of the Relay-supplied `id: GlobalID!`, unconditional `is_type_of` injection across all `DjangoType`s, composite-primary-key rejection with `ConfigurationError`, and both sync and async paths for node lookup — all with no new public exports and 100% package coverage held.

### Spec changes made (Worker 1 only)

- `docs/spec-relay_interfaces.md:3` — trimmed the now-stale "remains to be deleted as part of this slice" qualifier and the parenthetical naming of `-3.md` as the sole survivor; replaced with a one-clause sentence asserting all three superseded drafts (`-1.md`, `-2.md`, `-3.md`) have been deleted and this file is the single source of truth. Reason: `docs/spec-relay_interfaces-3.md` was deleted as part of Slice 5; Worker 3's review surfaced the line as the only remaining spec reconciliation candidate. Slice that triggered the change: Slice 5.

### Final status

`final-accepted`.
